abstract:
In this paper, we present BEVDepth, a high-performance 3D object detection framework designed to enhance the reliability of depth estimation in camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception. While depth is a fundamental component for projecting multi-view images into a 3D space, we observe that existing "lift-splat-shoot" paradigm detectors produced surprisingly inaccurate depth maps, often relying on only a fraction of correctly localized features to achieve reasonable detection results. 

To address this limitation, BEVDepth introduces explicit depth supervision using point cloud data, ensuring the model learns geometrically meaningful representations. We further incorporate a camera-aware depth estimation module that integrates extrinsic and intrinsic parameters directly into the depth prediction process, improving robustness across varying camera configurations. Additionally, we propose a Depth Refinement Module to mitigate errors introduced during feature unprojection. When combined with an optimized, high-speed voxel pooling operator and a multi-frame fusion strategy, BEVDepth sets a new benchmark in the field. It achieves a state-of-the-art 60.9% NDS on the nuScenes test set, representing the first time a camera-only model has surpassed the 60% NDS threshold while maintaining significant computational efficiency.
